response.description_long:
Serverless GPU inference platforms typically force a choice: keep expensive GPUs always-on or accept multi-minute cold starts while model weights load. Modelgrid addresses this with a snapshot architecture that checkpoints the fully-initialized model — weights, CUDA context, allocator state — into a distributed cache next to the GPUs. Restoring from a snapshot brings a model from zero to serving in under 2 seconds, even for models tens of gigabytes in size.

Modelgrid runs machine-learning models as autoscaling API endpoints. It supports any model with a PyTorch or vLLM serving path, including language models, embedding models, diffusion models, and speech-to-text. Users bring a container or Python function, and Modelgrid handles batching, queueing, autoscaling from zero, and GPU selection across A100 and H100 classes. The resulting HTTPS endpoint bills per second of compute, so a model that serves 10 minutes of traffic a day costs minutes, not hours.

The platform targets teams shipping AI features that face spiky or unpredictable traffic: overnight batch jobs, consumer apps with regional peaks, or agents that fan out to many models. By eliminating the cold-start penalty, Modelgrid lets these teams scale to zero when idle without sacrificing responsiveness.

Modelgrid was founded in San Francisco in 2023 by infrastructure engineers from a large GPU cloud. The company runs its own capacity across three US regions and publishes status and latency numbers openly. The founding insight — that model checkpointing can be extended to capture full GPU state — directly informed the product's technical approach.
